comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as a structured-concurrency / ownership issue. extensions/tau/src/autoresearch/index.ts still keeps long-lived loop workflow state in module-local Maps (activeAutoresearchLoops at line 889, waitingAutoresearchAgentEnds at 890) and starts the main loop via detached fire-and-forget async code in runAutoresearchLoop(): void (lines 1474-1573), specifically void (async () => { ... })() at line 1483. Cancellation is manual through ActiveAutoresearchLoopControl { cancelled, cancelWait } plus AbortController wiring for the current wait (1515-1523), and final cleanup removes the map entry in finally (1569-1571). That is better than a raw leak, but the workflow itself is still outside Effect-scoped fiber ownership. So the review claim is directionally valid: waiter cleanup improved, while the loop runner remains detached async state managed by Maps rather than a scoped Effect service.
